home.title: WMF Board Elections
home.subtitle: Interactive visualization of Wikimedia Foundation Board of Trustees elections — community-elected and affiliate-selected trustees, 2004–2025.
home.feature_elections: Full table of all elections 2004–2025 with filtering and side-by-side comparison.
home.feature_candidates: Profiles of all trustees ever elected — edit counts, countries, election history.
home.feature_map: World map showing geographic representation of candidates and winners.
home.feature_trends: Turnout, gender diversity, affiliate analysis, and long-term trends.
home.feature_board: Gantt-style timeline of all board terms — community, affiliate, and appointed.
home.feature_about: Background on voting systems, data sources, and methodology.

about.title: About this project
about.what_body: This tool visualizes data from elections to the Wikimedia Foundation Board of Trustees — the governing body of the Wikimedia Foundation (WMF), the non-profit organization behind Wikipedia and other Wikimedia projects. The Board sets strategic direction for WMF and is responsible for organizational oversight.
about.board_body: The Board has multiple types of members: community-elected trustees chosen directly by Wikimedia contributors, affiliate-selected trustees chosen by Wikimedia affiliate organizations, and appointed trustees brought in for specific expertise. The WMF CEO participates as a non-voting member.
about.data_body: Data covers community elections and affiliate-selected processes from 2005 to 2025. Voting systems have changed over the years: plurality (2005–2006), approval voting (2008–2016), and Single Transferable Vote (STV, 2021–present). All data is sourced from Meta-Wiki and publicly available election records.
about.stv_body: Single Transferable Vote (STV) is a ranked-choice voting system used since 2021. Voters rank candidates in order of preference. Votes are transferred when a candidate is elected with surplus votes or eliminated. The Sankey diagrams on election pages show how votes flowed across rounds.
about.methodology_body: Vote counts and turnout figures come from official election reports published on Meta-Wiki. Where exact ballot dumps are available, STV round data is reconstructed. Candidate experience data (edit counts, account age) reflects the time of the election.
about.source_body: All election data is published under CC BY-SA 4.0. Source: Meta-Wiki — Wikimedia Foundation elections.
about.contribute_body: This project is open source. Data is stored as TypeScript files in the repository. Corrections and additions are welcome via pull requests.

election_detail.sankey_note: Bars show vote count per round · Yellow border = elected · Red dashed line = quota

trends.title: Trends
trends.scatter_note: Each dot is one candidate–election entry. Hover for details.
